rationale: In the citing paper, Hildebrand et al. (2014) is used in core theory/method sections rather than only as background listing. The authors cite it to define by-attribute customization and to justify constructs relevant to interface design, and they adopt a key outcome measure (product satisfaction) from it. The citation is not used to dispute prior claims, but it provides meaningful conceptual and methodological grounding for the new culture-interface matching argument.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Hildebrand et al. (2014) as prior customization-interface work, using it to frame interface structure and related constructs. It also reuses operational elements from that study (notably the satisfaction scale) while developing a new cross-cultural matching theory.
